_meta.dna: esign(compliance) = attest(identity) x sign(document) x seal(hash) x chain(evidence) x retain(90d) x audit(trace) x export(package)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions from a specific persona context. Questions must be answerable by checking if something actually works — click it, see it, verify it. Not theoretical.

name: Tower of eSign + FDA Part 11
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 54, Boston, Quality Assurance Director at pharma company, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, needs FDA Part 11 compliant electronic signatures for batch release records
questions: Can I navigate to the eSign page and click 'Create Attestation' to start a new attestation with my authenticated identity?
Does the attestation form capture my full name, role, email, and timestamp as required by FDA 21 CFR Part 11?
Can I type a specific attestation statement ('I certify this batch release record is accurate') in a free-text field?
Does the attestation bind to a specific document or evidence bundle by reference ID — not just float as a standalone record?
After creating the attestation, can I see it listed in my attestation history with status 'Created' and a unique attestation ID?
Does the system prevent me from creating an attestation without first authenticating (no unsigned attestations possible)?
If I am logged in but my session token is expired, does the system re-authenticate me before allowing attestation creation?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Dr. Hawkins works under FDA regulations. Attestations must capture identity, bind to documents, and reject unauthenticated attempts. Missing any field is a regulatory violation.

floors.persona_context: 47, Philadelphia, Clinical Trial Manager, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, signs protocol amendments that must be legally binding
questions: After creating an attestation, can I click 'Sign' and see my digital signature applied with a SHA-256 hash of the document content?
Does the signature record include the signer's user ID, timestamp (UTC), IP address, and the hash of the signed content?
Can I verify that the signature hash matches the document content by running a verification check from the UI?
If I try to sign a document that has been modified since I last viewed it, does the system warn me about the content change?
Does the signed document show a visual signature indicator (signed badge, green checkmark) in the document list?
Can I download a signed PDF that includes the signature metadata embedded in the document properties?
If two people need to sign the same document (co-signature), does the system support sequential signing with separate hashes?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Jonathan signs legally binding documents. Digital signatures must be cryptographically verifiable, tamper-evident, and support multi-signer workflows.

floors.persona_context: 39, Tokyo, Compliance Engineer at medical device company, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, validates hash chain integrity for ISO 13485 audits
questions: After signing a document, can I see the hash chain showing this signature linked to the previous signature via parent hash?
Does the hash chain viewer display each record as a block with: hash, parent_hash, timestamp, action_type, and payload summary?
Can I click 'Verify Chain' and see a pass/fail result for the entire chain from genesis block to latest record?
If I manually corrupt one record's hash in the database, does the verification tool detect the break and show which record failed?
Does the sealing process use SHA-256 (not MD5 or SHA-1) as required by current cryptographic standards?
Can I view the raw JSON of any hash chain record to inspect the exact fields being hashed?
Does the hash chain include evidence of who sealed each record — preventing unauthorized sealing by non-signers?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Keiko validates cryptographic integrity for ISO audits. Hash chains must be verifiable, use strong algorithms, and detect any tampering. Weak hashing or missing chain links are audit failures.

floors.persona_context: 62, Dublin, Regulatory Affairs VP at biotech, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, needs 90-day minimum evidence retention with configurable extensions
questions: Can I configure the evidence retention policy to 90 days minimum via the admin settings panel?
Does the system prevent deletion of evidence records that are within the retention period — even by administrators?
After 90 days, does the system archive (not delete) expired evidence to cold storage that is still queryable?
Can I extend the retention period for specific evidence bundles beyond 90 days for ongoing audits?
Does the retention policy display show the exact expiry date for each evidence bundle?
If I am on Free tier (no cloud), does the system still enforce 90-day local retention and warn before disk cleanup?
Can I generate a retention compliance report showing all evidence bundles, their creation dates, and expiry status?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Margaret must demonstrate retention compliance to regulators. Retention must be enforced (not advisory), configurable, and produce audit-ready reports.

floors.persona_context: 45, Houston, FDA Inspector (external auditor role), Expert tech, audit access only, needs to query complete audit trails during facility inspections
questions: Can I search the audit trail by date range and see all attestations, signatures, and evidence records created in that period?
Does the audit trail show who did what, when, from which IP, and what the outcome was — for every action in the system?
Can I filter audit records by user to see all actions taken by a specific employee during a specific month?
Does the audit trail include failed actions (rejected signatures, failed attestations) — not just successful ones?
Can I export the complete audit trail for a date range as a single JSON or CSV file for offline analysis?
Does the audit trail include system events (token refresh, session timeout, config changes) — not just user actions?
Is the audit trail itself immutable — can I verify that no audit records have been deleted or modified?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Dr. Okonkwo is an external auditor. The audit trail must be comprehensive, immutable, filterable, and exportable. Missing or modifiable audit records are regulatory violations.

floors.persona_context: 38, Lyon, Quality Systems Manager, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, needs to create self-contained export packages for regulatory submissions
questions: Can I select a set of evidence records and click 'Export Package' to generate a self-contained ZIP file?
Does the export package include all documents, signatures, hash chains, and a verification manifest?
Can I open the export package on a different computer without Solace installed and verify the hash chain integrity?
Does the export package include a human-readable summary (HTML or PDF) alongside the machine-readable JSON?
Is the export package itself hash-signed so the recipient can verify it was not tampered with after export?
Can I export packages for specific date ranges, specific documents, or specific users — with filtering?
Does the export include the software version and configuration state at the time of signing for reproducibility?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Claire submits export packages to regulators. Packages must be self-contained, verifiable offline, human-readable, and tamper-evident.

floors.persona_context: 58, Washington DC, Regulatory consultant advising pharma clients, Expert tech, evaluating Solace for client recommendation, needs 21 CFR Part 11 checklist compliance
questions: Does the system satisfy FDA 21 CFR Part 11.10(a) — validation: is there documented evidence that the system performs as intended?
Does the system satisfy Part 11.10(c) — protection of records: can records be protected from unauthorized access or modification?
Does the system satisfy Part 11.10(e) — audit trail: does the system generate a computer-generated, timestamped audit trail?
Does the system satisfy Part 11.50 — signature manifestations: does each signature include the signer's name, date, time, and meaning?
Does the system satisfy Part 11.70 — signature/record linking: are electronic signatures linked to their respective records so signatures cannot be transferred?
Can I generate a Part 11 compliance matrix document from the system showing each requirement and its implementation status?
Does the system documentation include an ERES (Electronic Records/Electronic Signatures) policy template I can adapt for my client?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Dr. Engel advises pharma clients. He needs to map Solace features to specific CFR sections. Generic claims of 'compliance-ready' without specific section mapping are worthless.
